fresh_facts.claim: US Navy seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on April 19, 2026, after it allegedly evaded interception for over 6 hours en route to Bandar Abbas.
US Navy warships enforced a blockade on Iranian ports, marking the first interception since the blockade began the previous week.
Iran's joint military command vowed a response to the ship seizure including drone operations targeting US naval assets in the Strait of Hormuz and Sea of Oman.
Iran announced plans to close or heavily restrict the Strait of Hormuz in direct response to the US blockade.
Iran rejected upcoming talks in Pakistan, with Foreign Minister Abbas Aragchi citing US military actions as aggression and stating no plans for fresh talks.
President Trump held a phone call with Pakistan's army chief Asim Munir on April 20 to address Iran's strait closure threats.
Trump convened an urgent war cabinet meeting on April 22 amid the ceasefire's expiration.
Trump stated the US naval blockade on Iran remains 'in full force' until a deal is reached.
Prediction markets show low odds of Iranian regime collapse at 1% by April 30 and 3.2% by May 31.
VP JD Vance headed to Pakistan on Sunday for talks with Iranian delegations, with Trump warning Iran to 'take the deal or face destruction.'
Iran considered a temporary halt to Hormuz shipments to de-escalate following its recent blockade reversal.
Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf called hardline opponents of a US deal 'extremists' undermining talks.
No IRGC defections or Supreme Leader visibility changes were noted 100 days post-January crackdown.
The Reza Pahlavi succession market rose to 4.5% YES for June 30, up 0.5 points in the last 24 hours.
A Provisional Leadership Council consisting of Pezeshkian, Mohseni-Ejei, and Arafi remains in place following the prior killing of Supreme Leader Khamenei.
The Assembly of Experts was disrupted by an Israeli strike in Qom on March 3, delaying succession proceedings.
Kuwait declared force majeure on shipments due to the Hormuz blockade disrupting oil flows.
GOP senators urged Trump on April 17 for an Iran exit plan as rising US energy prices prompted concerns over the Hormuz blockade.
The Iranian government backed off compensating homeowners hit by US-Israeli strikes, sparking public anger.
Trump characterized Iran's actions as a 'total violation' of ceasefire terms on April 20.
Iran called Trump 'erratic' in response to threats after the Hormuz reversal on April 20.
Pakistan pushed for diplomatic talks scheduled for the week of April 20 between US and Iranian delegations.
CSIS analysis noted that the fragile ceasefire hides unresolved nuclear and terrorism risks.
IRGC forces fired on a tanker on Saturday after briefly reopening the Strait of Hormuz before reimposing the blockade.
Analysts warned of rapid escalation if vessel attacks continue, with Al Jazeera noting escalation 12 hours after the April 19 seizure.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces a multi-front crisis spanning April-June 2026: a failed ceasefire punctuated by US naval blockade enforcement (Touska seizure on April 19), Iranian retaliation via drone strikes, escalating Hormuz control claims, comprehensive Israeli strikes on June 14-15 eliminating IRGC leadership and energy infrastructure, cascading internal instability with mandatory Basij deployment, succession upheaval from March 3 Assembly strike, economic freefall, and weakened regional proxy networks following Assad's fall.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli airstrikes on June 14-15 killed IRGC Intelligence Chief Mohammad Kazemi and deputies while destroying critical energy infrastructure including Shahran oil depot, regime ministries, and airbases, simultaneously degrading coercive security apparatus capacity and forcing emergency Basij nationwide deployment to suppress dissent.
